sentence: Once there was a queen who had a little daughter, too young to walk on her own.

sentence: Long afterward, a man was walking through the forest when he heard a raven’s call and followed the sound.

sentence: “What must I do?”

sentence: She will offer you food and drink, but you must not accept either, or you will fall into a deep sleep and won’t be able to help me.

sentence: For three days in a row, I will come in my carriage at two o’clock in the afternoon.

sentence: But if you fall asleep any day, I won’t be freed.”

sentence:
I only thought of eating you because I was so hungry.”

Inside, the man brought out the endless bread, meat, and wine.
